title: Jessie Buckley's Golden Globe Victory
text: Irish actress Jessie Buckley won Best Actress in a Drama at the 83rd Golden Globe Awards for her performance in Hamnet. The historical drama, based on Maggie O'Farrell's bestselling novel, also captured Best Picture in the Drama category. During her acceptance speech at the Beverly Hilton in Los Angeles, Buckley thanked director Chloé Zhao and her co-star Paul Mescal. She acknowledged her fellow nominees, saying: Julia Roberts, you are, like, a hero to us all, thank you. She also praised actresses Jennifer Lawrence, Renate Reinsve, Eva Victor, and Tessa Thompson. The Killarney-born performer concluded by expressing her appreciation for the industry: This is a real, real honour. I love what I do, and I love being part of this industry. Hamnet tells the story of Agnes and William Shakespeare's deceased son, featuring an international cast and crew from multiple countries. The film's success came as a surprise to many observers who had predicted the supernatural horror Sinners would win Best Picture. Other major winners included the Netflix crime drama Adolescence and comedy thriller One Battle After Another, each securing four awards.
